title: Why Are We Here? Why Did We Raise $15B?
body: Andreessen Horowitz announcement on fund raising and mission
Positioning for American technological leadership across AI, crypto, and critical sectors

title: Overview: Capital Deployment and Strategic Vision
body: Over $15B raised across six major fund categories
Represents over 18% of all U.S. venture capital allocated in 2025
Focus areas: AI, crypto, biology, health, defense, and education
Strategic response to intensifying competition with China

title: American Dynamism Fund
body: $1.176B allocated to American Dynamism strategy
Part of broader portfolio supporting national competitiveness
Aligned with defense and critical infrastructure priorities

title: Apps Fund Allocation
body: $1.7B raised for Apps fund
Represents significant bet on application-layer innovation
One of the largest single-category allocations in the portfolio

title: Bio + Health Investment Strategy
body: $700M dedicated to Bio + Health fund
Applying AI and technological advances to biology and health sectors
Part of mission to maintain American leadership in critical life sciences

title: Infrastructure Fund
body: $1.7B allocated to Infrastructure strategy
Matches Apps fund as one of largest single-category commitments
Foundational to supporting AI and technological capabilities

title: Growth Fund: Largest Single Allocation
body: $6.75B raised for Growth fund
Represents 45% of total capital raised
Largest individual fund category in the portfolio
Signals commitment to scaling proven technology companies

title: Other Strategies Portfolio
body: $3B allocated across other strategies beyond the five named funds
Provides flexibility for emerging opportunities and specialized investments
Represents 20% of total capital deployment

title: Market Position: 18% of U.S. Venture Capital
body: Over 18% of all U.S. venture capital allocated in 2025
Unprecedented market share for a single venture firm
Reflects concentration of capital in established platform firms
Positions firm as systemic player in American innovation ecosystem

title: Mission: Opportunity Regardless of Background
body: Ben Horowitz frames the mission as providing opportunity regardless of background
Connects venture investment to broader American promise of social mobility
Positions capital deployment as continuation of 250-year tradition

title: Historical Context: 250 Years of American Flourishing
body: America's system has produced unprecedented human flourishing over 250 years
Venture capital positioned as extension of American exceptionalism
Framework connects economic model to historical national success

title: Technology Leadership Imperative
body: Firm positions itself as responsible for American technological leadership in AI and crypto
Leadership mandate extends to biology, health, defense, and education applications
Technology dominance framed as existential national priority

title: Strategic Competition with China
body: Warning that losing technological dominance risks economic leadership
Military, geopolitical, and cultural leadership also at stake
Competition with China intensifying across all dimensions
Technology leadership framed as foundation for maintaining American primacy

title: Outlook: Technology as National Security
body: Venture capital positioned as instrument of national strategy
AI and crypto identified as critical battleground technologies
$15B deployment framed as response to great power competition
American technological leadership presented as non-negotiable priority
